skill.name: jira-cli
skill.description: Use jira-cli to manage Jira issues, epics, sprints, boards, comments, work logs, and workflow transitions from an AI-assisted command line.
skill.summary: Manage Jira issues and sprint work with jira-cli command guidance.
skill.author: Code-and-Sorts

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are mostly Markdown examples for the intended jira-cli workflow, not embedded executable code. The skill is publishable, but it can guide agents to run commands that create, edit, transition, comment on, worklog, clone, and delete Jira issues. Network URL and weak-crypto alerts are false positives from documentation links, front matter, and command text.

medium_findings.title: Jira CLI Commands Can Modify Remote Project Data
medium_findings.description: The skill documents jira-cli commands for creating, editing, moving, assigning, commenting, logging work, linking, cloning, and deleting Jira issues. This is expected functionality, but an agent using it can change production Jira data if the user account has permission.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced sections contain direct jira-cli examples that mutate Jira issues. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but the operational impact is clear.

medium_findings.title: Jira API Token Required For Authenticated Operations
medium_findings.description: The skill requires a JIRA_API_TOKEN environment variable and jira init configuration. No exfiltration is shown, but users must protect the token because CLI commands authenticate to Jira.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token requirement is explicitly documented. I found no evidence that the skill reads or transmits the token outside normal jira-cli use.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links To External GitHub Resources
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the upstream jira-cli project and its release page. They are expected documentation links, not hidden outbound requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible Markdown links to GitHub. I found no embedded code that contacts those URLs automatically.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Crypto Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography lines are Markdown metadata, Jira command examples, and flag-table entries. I found no hashing or cryptographic API usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines contain ordinary text and Jira CLI arguments. No weak algorithm such as MD5 or SHA1 is used as code.

low_findings.title: PowerShell Alert Is A Shell Completion Reference
low_findings.description: The PowerShell finding is from a comment listing supported completion shells. It does not invoke PowerShell or include a PowerShell script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 231 only names powershell as one completion option. There is no PowerShell command body or execution instruction.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples In Markdown Code Blocks
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains many jira-cli shell examples. They are not automatically executed by the skill file, but an agent may run them when fulfilling user requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code blocks are clearly command examples for the intended CLI. The risk depends on agent execution context and Jira permissions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution In Assignee Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Several examples use command substitution to resolve the current Jira user. The substituted command is fixed as jira me, which reduces injection risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell substitution pattern is real, but the inner command is static. I found no user-controlled substitution payload.

content.user_title: Manage Jira Work From the CLI
content.value_statement: Jira work often requires switching between tools and screens. This skill provides concise jira-cli guidance for issue, sprint, epic, and board workflows.
actual_capabilities: Lists Jira issues with assignee, status, priority, date, and JQL filters.
Creates issues with type, summary, body, priority, labels, components, and assignee options.
Views, edits, assigns, comments on, links, clones, moves, and deletes issues.
Logs work time and completes common status-transition workflows.
Lists and manages epics, sprints, boards, projects, and releases.
Explains useful jira-cli output modes, including plain text, raw JSON, and CSV.
limitations: Requires jira-cli to be installed and configured before use.
Requires a valid Jira API token and account permissions.
Some commands vary between Jira Cloud, Jira Server, and custom workflows.
Complex custom fields may require manual field IDs and extra flags.

use_cases.title: Triage Sprint Work
use_cases.description: List current sprint issues, filter by assignee or priority, and inspect details before standup.

use_cases.title: Update Assigned Tickets
use_cases.description: Assign issues, move statuses, add comments, and log work without opening the Jira web interface.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Tracking
use_cases.description: Review projects, boards, releases, epics, and linked issues while planning delivery milestones.

prompt_templates.title: List My Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my Jira issues in the current sprint and highlight anything marked High priority.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Bug
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a High priority Jira bug for a login crash with a short description and the bug label.

prompt_templates.title: Move And Comment
prompt_templates.prompt: Move ISSUE-123 to Done, add a completion comment, and set the resolution to Fixed.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Epic Work
prompt_templates.prompt: List active epics, show open issues for the selected epic, and suggest jira-cli commands to update sprint placement.

output_examples.output: A concise list of assigned Jira issues with keys, summaries, statuses, and priorities, plus commands used to retrieve them.

output_examples.input: Create a task for dependency updates.
output_examples.output: A proposed Jira task summary, labels, component choice, priority, and the exact confirmation needed before creating it.
A short workflow plan covering worklog entry, status transition, completion comment, and resolution setting.

best_practices: Confirm issue keys, project keys, and destructive operations before running mutation commands.
Use plain output for review tasks and CSV output for spreadsheet exports.
Store Jira API tokens securely and avoid pasting tokens into prompts or comments.
anti_patterns: Do not run delete or move commands without explicit user confirmation.
Do not expose Jira API tokens, account IDs, or private issue data in shared logs.
Do not assume every Jira instance uses the same statuses, priorities, or custom fields.

faq.question: Does this skill install jira-cli?
faq.answer: No. It provides command guidance. The user must install jira-cli and run jira init first.

faq.question: Can it create Jira issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents jira-cli commands for interactive and noninteractive issue creation.

faq.question: Can it change existing issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide edits, assignments, comments, worklogs, links, clones, and workflow transitions.

faq.question: Does it need network access?
faq.answer: The skill file has only documentation links, but jira-cli needs network access to your Jira instance.

faq.question: Does it handle Jira custom fields?
faq.answer: Only at a basic guidance level. Complex custom fields may need manual field IDs and extra flags.

faq.question: Is it safe for production Jira projects?
faq.answer: It can be used safely with review steps, but mutation commands should require explicit confirmation.
